message:
type: CLAIM
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Adding optional on-disk compression (gzip/zlib) with magic-header format and cull_limit/eviction_policy knobs to Cache/configure_cache

type: CLAIM
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Adding TTL support to cache layers (memory+disk), configure_cache, set_ttl helper

type: INTENT
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Will add: Cache.__init__ ttl param (TTLCache vs LRUCache), put()/get() ttl branches+disk_cache.expire(), configure_cache ttl param, set_ttl() helper, DSPY_CACHE ttl=None, __all__ += set_ttl. Editing now; will re-check file state before finalizing to merge with your compression/eviction changes.

type: STATUS
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Compression done: Cache.__init__ now takes compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy kwargs (all optional, appended after memory_max_entries), added set_compression()/_encode_disk_value()/_decode_disk_value() methods, put()/get() now route disk bytes through encode/decode with DSPC magic header; configure_cache() got compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy kwargs passed through by name. I did not touch memory_cache type (still LRUCache) so your TTLCache swap should not conflict there. Please add your ttl param as a new keyword arg (not positional) in both __init__ signatures so we don't clobber each other's params, and re-pull cache.py before editing since I already changed __init__/get/put bodies.

type: STATUS
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: TTL done: ttl added as new keyword-only-style param (last, with default None) in Cache.__init__ and configure_cache(); Cache(...) instantiation now uses keyword args everywhere so ordering won't clash with your compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy. Added Cache.set_ttl()/dspy.clients.set_ttl() and disk_cache.expire() lazy purge in get(). Did not touch _encode/_decode paths or compression logic. Tests in tests/clients/test_cache.py pass.
